zh.Ryme Intrinsica Liberty, Dorset, England: 英國多瑟特音特林希卡 利波迪
zh.Ryme Instrinseca, Dorset, England: 英國多瑟特音斯特林瑟卡
zh.lifeSummary: 當John de Beauchamp於大約1315年出生在英國多瑟特音斯特林瑟卡時，他的父親Sir John Beauchamp42歲，母親Joan Nonant25歲。他於1340年與Margaret Whalesburgh在英國多瑟特音斯特林瑟卡結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於大約1348年4月8日在他的家鄉享年34歲。

zh-hans.Ryme Intrinsica Liberty, Dorset, England: 英国多瑟特音特林希卡 利波迪
zh-hans.Ryme Instrinseca, Dorset, England: 英国多瑟特音斯特林瑟卡
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当John de Beauchamp于大约1315年出生在英国多瑟特音斯特林瑟卡时，他的父亲Sir John Beauchamp42岁，母亲Joan Nonant25岁。他于1340年与Margaret Whalesburgh在英国多瑟特音斯特林瑟卡结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他去世于大约1348年4月8日在他的家乡享年34岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Beauchamp
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Antoine, Jacques, Yves, Andre, Armand, Emile, Fernand, Marcel, Marthe, Remi, Alcide, Camille.

English (of Norman origin) and French: habitational name from any of several places in France, for example in Manche and Somme, that are named with Old French beu, bel ‘fair, lovely’ + champ(s) ‘field, plain’ (from Latin bello campo ‘beautiful field’). In English the surname is generally pronounced Beecham . Compare Bushaw .
English: in some cases, an Anglo-Norman French adaptation of Scottish Campbell , by translation into French of the folk etymology de campo bello ‘from the fair field’.

variants.name: Beecham
meanings: English (Lincolnshire): variant of   Beauchamp  , reflecting the normal English pronunciation.

variants.name: Beachem
meanings: English (Somerset and Gloucestershire): variant of   Beauchamp  .

variants.name: Beachum
meanings: Altered form of English   Beauchamp  .

variants.name: Bagent
meanings: English:
possibly a colloquial pronunciation of Beauchamp or Beecham . Old French Beau- often appears as Be(e)- in English surnames. The -ch- has been voiced and excrescent -t has been added.

perhaps an Anglicized form of a French or Flemish surname such as Beaujean and Bejean, from Old French bel Jehan ‘handsome John’ + excrescent -t. It could have been introduced to England in the 15th or 16th century by Flemish migrants.

variants.name: Beacham
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Beauchamp  .

variants.name: Campbell
meanings: Scottish: nickname from Gaelic cam ‘crooked, bent’ + beul ‘mouth’. As a result of folk etymology, the surname was often represented in Latin documents as de bello campo ‘of the fair field’, which led to the name sometimes being ‘translated’ into Anglo-Norman French as Beauchamp .
Irish (North Armagh): adopted for Gaelic Mac Cathmhaoil ‘son of Cathmhaol’ (literally ‘battle chief’): see Caulfield and Cowell .
English: variant of   Camel  , under the influence of the Scottish name (see 1 above).

variants.name: Bushaw
meanings: Americanized form of French   Beauchamp   and   Bouchard  .

variants.name: Beacom
meanings: English (Somerset) and Irish (Fermanagh): of Norman origin, a variant of   Beauchamp  .

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When John de Beauchamp was born about 1315, in Ryme Instrinseca, Dorset, England, his father, Sir John Beauchamp, was 42 and his mother, Joan Nonant, was 25. He married Margaret Whalesburgh in 1340, in Ryme Instrinseca, Dorset, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 2 daughters. He died about 8 April 1348, in his hometown, at the age of 34.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque John de Beauchamp est né environ 1315, à Ryme Instrinseca, Dorset, Angleterre, son père, Sir John Beauchamp, avait 42 ans et sa mère, Joan Nonant, avait 25 ans. Il a épousé Margaret Whalesburgh en 1340, à Ryme Instrinseca, Dorset, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé environ 8 avril 1348, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 34 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als John de Beauchamp um 1315 in Ryme Instrinseca, Dorset, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir John Beauchamp 42 und seine Mutter Joan Nonant 25. Er hat 1340 in Ryme Instrinseca, Dorset, England Margaret Whalesburgh geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 2 Töchter. Er starb um 8. April 1348 im Alter von 34 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando John de Beauchamp è nato il circa 1315 nel Ryme Instrinseca, Dorset, England, il suo padre, Sir John Beauchamp, aveva 42 anni e il suo madre, Joan Nonant, aveva 25 anni. Lui sposato Margaret Whalesburgh nel 1340, nel Ryme Instrinseca, Dorset, England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 2 figlie. Morì circa 8 aprile 1348, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 34 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: John de Beauchampが約1315年にイギリスドールセトリメ インストリンセカで生まれた時，父Sir John Beauchampは42歳，母Joan Nonantは25歳であった。1340年にイギリスドールセトリメ インストリンセカでMargaret Whalesburghと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は約1348年4月8日に故郷で34歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: John de Beauchamp(이)가 대략 1315년 영국 도르세트 리 인스트린세카에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir John Beauchamp(은)는 42세였고, 그의 어머니 Joan Nonant(은)는 25세였다. 그는 1340년 영국 도르세트 리 인스트린세카에서 Margaret Whalesburgh(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 대략 1348년 4월 8일 그의 고향에서 34세의 나이로 사망했다.

mg.about 1315–about 8 April 1348: tany ho any amin'ny 1315–tany ho any amin'ny 8 aprily 1348

pt.lifeSummary: Quando John de Beauchamp nasceu aproximadamente 1315, em Ryme Instrinseca, Dorset, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir John Beauchamp, tinha 42 anos e sua mãe, Joan Nonant, tinha 25 anos. Ele casou-se com Margaret Whalesburgh em 1340, em Ryme Instrinseca, Dorset, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 2 filhas. Ele faleceu aproximadamente 8 de abril de 1348, em sua cidade natal, com 34 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда John de Beauchamp родился около 1315 г. в местности рим инстринсека, дорсет, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir John Beauchamp было 42 года, а матери, которую звали Joan Nonant, 25. Он женился на женщине по имени Margaret Whalesburgh в 1340 г. в местности рим инстринсека, дорсет, Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 2 дочери. Он умер около 8 апреля 1348 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 34 лет.

sm.about 1315–about 8 April 1348: e tusa o le 1315–e tusa o le 8 Aperila 1348

es.lifeSummary: Cuando John de Beauchamp nació aproximadamente 1315, en Ryme Instrinseca, Dorset, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir John Beauchamp, tenía 42 años y su mamá, Joan Nonant, tenía 25 años. Se casó con Margaret Whalesburgh en 1340, en Ryme Instrinseca, Dorset, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 2 hijas. Murió aproximadamente 8 de abril de 1348, en su pueblo, a la edad de 34 años.
